title: React Navigation Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on Stack routing, physical screens, Bottom Tabs, and Drawers.

questions.question: In standard web development using `react-router-dom`, navigating to a new URL structurally unmounts the old page entirely. How does mobile 'Stack Routing' heavily differ conceptually from this approach?

options.text: It forces the application into an HTML WebView.
It uses raw string IDs to navigate.

When moving forward, it literally physically 'stacks' the brand-new Screen directly on top of the old one in a massive pile. The old Screen is NOT destroyed; it quietly persists faithfully executing underneath.
It deletes the app entirely and restarts it natively.

questions.question: What happens precisely to the underlying `HomeScreen` in a Stack Navigator when a user triggers `navigation.navigate('SettingsScreen')`?

options.text: The `HomeScreen` is instantly garbage collected strictly to completely save system RAM.
The `SettingsScreen` physically slides securely into view entirely on top of the `HomeScreen`, preserving the original `HomeScreen`'s precise state seamlessly in the background.
The mobile app pauses completely.
The `HomeScreen` explicitly converts into `SettingsScreen` code.

questions.question: When a user executes `navigation.goBack()` explicitly on iOS, how does the Stack Navigator physically react?

options.text: It gracefully tosses the top-most active Screen permanently into the garbage (popping the stack), smoothly revealing the impeccably preserved underlying Screen immediately.
It crashes the application silently.
It reloads the data entirely from the remote server API.
It generates a completely new instance from scratch.

questions.question: Which specific Navigator precisely renders a permanent menu interface securely hugging the absolute bottom edge of the phone display containing discrete interactive icons?

options.text: Drawer Navigator
Stack Navigator
Switch Navigator
Bottom Tab Navigator

questions.question: What is structurally possible regarding nesting complex navigators deep inside one another utilizing the React Navigation framework?

options.text: You fundamentally cannot nest Navigators; doing so will explicitly crash the UI.
You can seamlessly nest an entire massive, infinite Stack Navigator exclusively deep inside a single specific tab of a Bottom Tab Navigator effortlessly.
You can only nest a single Navigator deeply on standard Tuesdays.
It requires writing custom complex Java code.
